QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_39

question.text: After how many years in NPS can a subscriber make a partial withdrawal?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Partial withdrawal from NPS Tier I is allowed after completing 3 years of subscription. **The Trap:** 5 years is not the requirement. 3 years is the minimum period before the first partial withdrawal can be made. **Cross-Exam Context:** Partial withdrawal rules: 3 years minimum, max 25% of own contributions, max 3 times during entire subscription, specific purposes only. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Inform clients about the 3-year waiting period upfront so they maintain emergency funds outside NPS for the initial years.
